message:
re-apply: avoid premature mission workflow completion events

This restores the behavior of commit 23e57dfe ("review: avoid
premature mission workflow completion events") which was
inadvertently reverted by commit 57cc3c0c ("mux: preserve early tmux
backlog chunks"). The mux commit touched `mission_dispatch.rs` —
presumably because it rebased a mux/tmux branch onto a base that
predated 23e57dfe and the stale file came along as collateral.

## The underlying bug (why 23e57dfe existed)

`MissionDispatcher::dispatch` publishes a `WorkflowCompleted` event
after a dispatch. The pre-fix code unconditionally emitted one of
two synthetic events:

    if result.accepted {
        bus.publish(WorkflowCompleted { success: true, ... });
    } else {
        bus.publish(WorkflowCompleted { success: false, ... });
    }

But an "accepted" dispatch only means the workflow runner accepted
the detection contract and scheduled a pane run — execution has NOT
completed yet. Synthesizing `success: true` on dispatch fires
downstream "work completed" consumers (metrics increments, mission
close-out, operator notifications) before any pane I/O has actually
happened. The `success: false` branch is fine because a rejected
dispatch really is terminal for that contract.

## The fix

Route through a small helper that maps a `DispatchResult` to an
`Option<Event>`:

    fn make_completion_event_for_result(...) -> Option<Event> {
        if result.accepted { None }
        else { Some(WorkflowCompleted { success: false, ... }) }
    }

`None` means "don't publish yet — the workflow engine will publish
its own completion event when pane execution actually finishes."

## Test coverage (restored)

- `accepted_dispatch_does_not_emit_completion_event` — asserts that
  an accepted dispatch yields `None`, guarding against a future
  regression of the same shape.
- `rejected_dispatch_emits_failure_completion_event` — asserts the
  rejected-path event is emitted with the original reason preserved.

## Why this is safe to re-apply

- The fix diff is byte-identical to 23e57dfe's diff against the
  pre-fix file contents. It is a true re-application, not a rewrite.
- The mux commit 57cc3c0c did not add any new logic to the
  mission_dispatch surface — it only deleted the 23e57dfe additions.
  Re-applying cannot conflict with an intentional mux-commit change
  because there was none.
- No change to `frankenterm/mux/src/tmux.rs` (the legitimate content
  of 57cc3c0c); that file is untouched by this commit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
